settings.title: Getting Ahead of Tick and Flea Season
settings.excerpt: Warmer weeks bring the parasites back, and a little planning now saves a lot of scratching later.
settings.body:
As the ground warms up, fleas and ticks come back with it. Every spring we see a wave of itchy, uncomfortable pets that could have been spared with timely prevention.

Now is the moment to get your animal's treatment up to date, before the problem takes hold rather than after. Prevention is cheaper, kinder, and far less stressful than treating an infestation.

Why now rather than later

Once fleas are in the house they are a real job to clear, because most of the population is not on your pet at all. It is in the carpet, the bedding, and the cracks in the floor. Getting ahead of the season means you deal with a tube of treatment rather than a full home clean.

If you walk in long grass or woodland, ticks deserve real attention too, since they can carry serious disease.

A few simple habits

Alongside regular treatment, a little routine goes a long way:

  • Run your hands over your pet after country walks, checking ears and toes.
  • Wash bedding warm and often through the spring.
  • Keep treatment on schedule rather than waiting for a sign of trouble.

We are running quick, no fuss parasite check appointments through April and May. Pop in and we will sort the right plan for your pet.
